titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the William A. Henry Papers

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday January 8, 2025 at 23:23

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This collection consists of correspondence, writings, research files, professional papers, awards, artwork, childhood papers, scrapbooks, memorials, condolence letters, audiovisual materials, and computer media documenting the work and life of critic and journalist William A. Henry III. Writings consist of corrected proofs for The Great One: The Life and Legend of Jackie Gleason (Doubleday, 1992) and In Defense of Elitism (Doubleday, 1994); scripts and other documentation from "Bob Fosse: Steam Heat," and shorter writings.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The William A. Henry Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the auhors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.
Box 29 (audiovisual materials): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Boxes 30, 31 (computer media): Restricted fragile material. Access copies of computer files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: William A. Henry Papers. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into nine series: I. Correspondence, 1966-1994. II. Writings, 1981-1994. III. Professional papers, 1979-1994. IV. Artwork, 1982, undated. V. Awards, 1966-1995. VI. Memorials and condolence letters, 1994-1995. VII. Personal papers, 1950-1971. VIII. Audiovisual materials, 1983-1994. IX. Computer media, 1993-1994.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection or revisions in arrangement and description.

bioghist.head: William A. Henry
bioghist.p: William A. "Bill" Henry, III (1950-1994) was an American journalist and cultural critic. A 1971 graduate of Yale University, Henry wrote for the Boston Globe in the 1970s, earning Pulitzer Prizes in 1975 for coverage of school desegregation in Boston and in 1980 for television criticism. In the 1980s Henry was an art critic for Time. He wrote three books, Visions of America: How We Saw the 1984 Election (Atlantic Monthly Press, 1985), The Great One: The Life and Legend of Jackie Gleason (Doubleday, 1992), and In Defense of Elitism (Doubleday, 1994), and won an Emmy in 1990 for Steam Heat, a video documentary on Bob Fosse. He passed away from a heart attack June 28, 1994, just before the publication of In Defense of Elitism .

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Letters from notable persons are arranged alphabetically by correspondent. General correspondence is in the order in which it was received.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence consists of letters to William A. Henry from notable correspondents, including Clive Barker, Tom Brokaw, Lloyd Richards, Neil Simon, Garry Trudeau, and others, and general correspondence.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three subseries: Books, 1991-1994; Great Performances: Dance in America, "Bob Fosse: Steam Heat", 1987-1990; and Shorter writings, 1981-1994.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The writings series consists of foul matter for The Great One: The Life and Legend of Jackie Gleason (Doubleday, 1992), research materials and foul matter for In Defense of Elitism (Doubleday, 1994), scripts, notes, printed material, and other papers relating to Great Performances: Dance in America, "Bob Fosse: Steam Heat," articles, essays, and other writings by William A. Henry.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The books subseries consists of foul matter for The Great One: The Life and Legend of Jackie Gleason (Doubleday, 1992) and research materials and foul matter for In Defense of Elitism (Doubleday, 1994).

did.unittitle: Great one: the life and legend of Jackie Gleason: foul matter
Great one: the life and legend of Jackie Gleason: foul matter
Great one: the life and legend of Jackie Gleason: foul matter
In defense of elitism: research files
In defense of elitism: research files
In defense of elitism: research files
In defense of elitism: foul matter
In defense of elitism: foul matter
Great Performances: Dance in America, "Bob Fosse: Steam Heat"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries consists of scripts, notes, printed material, and other papers relating to Great Performances: Dance in America, "Bob Fosse: Steam Heat," which Henry wrote.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Shorter writings consist of articles, essays, and introductions by William A. Henry.

did.unittitle: American institutions and the media. Introduction
Is TV getting better or worse?
Rape and the courtroom: the victim on trial

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Professional papers consist of contracts, records of appearances, an address list, photographs, printed material, and other papers.

did.unittitle: Books to which William A. Henry III contributed
New York Drama Critics Circle address list

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of three pieces of art owned by William A. Henry.

did.unittitle: Lard, M. G.: untitled ink drawing

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of awards recognizing Henry's work as a journalist and critic, as well as a scrapbook of clippings related to Henry's 1980 Pulitzer Prize for television criticism.

did.unittitle: Emmy award: Dance in America. Bob Fosse. Steam Heat
GLAAD media award outstanding feature writer
Lowell Mellett Award: Journalism under fire
New England Associated Press Editors' first place
New Jersey Literary Hall of Fame
New Jersey Literary Hall of Fame
North Plainfield High Scholl Alumni Hall of Fame first annual induction and awards ceremony: program
North Plainfield High Scholl Alumni Hall of Fame first annual induction and awards: certificate
Ohio State award: Bob Fosse. Steam Heat
Pulitzer Prize 75th anniversary Columbia University: award

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Memorials and condolence letters consist of correspondence received by Henry's widow Gail after his passing, papers related to his memorial service, and a scrapbook of obituaries.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Personal papers consist of a baby book, childhood school papers, a scrapbook, and papers from Henry's time as an undergraduate at Yale.

did.unittitle: George Farquhar and the rise of naturalism

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted Fragile Material. Reference copies of audiovisual materials may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Audiocassette recordings of radio and other appearances by Bill Henry constitute the bulk of audiovisual materials. Also includes audiocassette recordings from Henry's memorial and a videocassette recording of "Bob Fosse Steam Heat," which was produced by Henry.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Audiovisual material: Restricted fragile. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
